Evidence and claim are inverted.","R1.7":"Hassanpour & Mehta (1987) is cited for an O(N^{-2}) convergence theorem derived under a 'deep ReLU ansatz' — deep ReLU network theory as a formal discipline did not exist in 1987; this citation is anachronistic and almost certainly fabricated, undermining the entire theoretical motivation section."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.2: The Results section states ResAdapt achieves residual 4.7×10⁻² vs. the uniform baseline's 8.1×10⁻³; 0.047 > 0.0081, so the baseline is strictly lower (better), yet the text declares ResAdapt the winner — a direct logical inversion of the numbers the authors themselves report.
rationale.R1.3: The interpretive claim that the residual gap is 'direct evidence that ResAdapt better satisfies the underlying Burgers' PDE' is flatly contradicted by the reported values: the adaptive scheme's residual is ~5.8× larger than the baseline, not smaller. Evidence and claim are inverted.
rationale.R1.7: Hassanpour & Mehta (1987) is cited for an O(N^{-2}) convergence theorem derived under a 'deep ReLU ansatz' — deep ReLU network theory as a formal discipline did not exist in 1987; this citation is anachronistic and almost certainly fabricated, undermining the entire theoretical motivation section.
